mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.77% is substantially below the current inflation rate (CPI at 291.474, implying ~8% YoY inflation). This negative real rate signals monetary accommodation that is insufficient to combat inflation. The Fed will be forced to raise rates aggressively over the next 20 trading days, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants price in higher future short rates and inflation expectations remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative as of June 2022
regime_hint: high inflation with monetary accommodation lag
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 20-day forward window despite negative real rates, or FEDFUNDS does not rise by at least 25 basis points within 60 days of trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 3.6% is near historical lows, indicating a tight labor market. Tight labor markets drive wage inflation, which forces the Fed to maintain or accelerate rate hikes. This persistent labor market tightness signals sustained upward pressure on Treasury yields as the Fed commits to restrictive policy to cool demand and labor market heat.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of May 2022, near 50-year lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over the 20-day window despite UNRATE remaining below 4.0%, or UNRATE rises above 4.5% within 30 days, signaling labor market deterioration that would reduce Fed tightening pressure.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 672,874M) remain elevated despite recent Fed tightening, indicating persistent consumer demand. Sustained demand pressures keep inflation expectations elevated and force the Fed to maintain restrictive policy. This demand resilience signals that Treasury yields will remain elevated or rise further as the Fed signals commitment to fighting inflation through higher rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales remain strong at 672,874M as of May 2022
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 5-day window despite RSAFS remaining above 670,000M, or RSAFS falls below 650,000M in the next monthly report, signaling demand destruction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 105.5973) shows supply-side constraints persist. Constrained supply combined with elevated demand creates persistent inflation that the Fed must combat with higher rates. This supply-demand imbalance signals that Treasury yields will rise as the Fed maintains restrictive policy and inflation expectations remain sticky.
evidence_refs: fact:Supply constraints persist while demand remains elevated
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window despite the supply-demand imbalance trigger being active, or INDPRO rises above 107.0 within 60 days, signaling supply normalization that would reduce inflation pressure.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,724K) have declined from recent peaks, signaling demand destruction in the housing sector. This decline indicates that Fed rate hikes are beginning to bite into demand. As demand destruction spreads, inflation expectations will moderate, allowing Treasury yields to fall as the market prices in a potential Fed pause or pivot in the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 1,724K as of April 2022, declining trend
regime_hint: demand destruction phase following monetary tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over the 20-day window despite HOUST remaining below 1,750K, or HOUST rebounds above 1,800K within 30 days, signaling housing demand resilience.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 151,682K) is elevated but growth is moderating as Fed tightening takes effect. Moderating employment growth signals that demand destruction is beginning, which will eventually reduce inflation pressure. This employment moderation triggers a market repricing of terminal rate expectations downward, causing Treasury yields to fall over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll employment at 151,682K as of May 2022
regime_hint: early-stage demand destruction with employment moderation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises over the 20-day window despite PAYEMS growth remaining below 0.3% MoM, or PAYEMS growth accelerates above 0.5% MoM in the next report.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL at 291.474) and a severely negative real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.77%) creates an unsustainable policy stance. Market participants will anticipate an aggressive Fed tightening cycle that must push real rates into positive territory. This anticipation of sustained rate hikes drives 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market prices in a prolonged restrictive policy regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Negative real rates with tight labor market and high inflation
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in high-inflation, tight-labor-market regime
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window despite all three trigger conditions being met, or FEDFUNDS rises above 3.0% within 60 days without corresponding Treasury yield increases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 19,731.119B) remains robust, but combined with elevated inflation and tight labor markets, this signals an economy running above potential. An overheating economy forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets initially priced in. This realization of persistent overheating drives 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market extends its forecast of elevated terminal rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP remains elevated while inflation persists
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window despite GDPC1 remaining above 19,700B and CPIAUCSL above 290, or GDPC1 contracts in the next quarterly release, signaling recession risk that would lower terminal rate expectations.
